description: Checkpoint 2 output schema for forward Lab CP2 and Oracle hindsight reconstruction. Preserves one shared CP2-core contract while allowing Oracle-only meta trace fields.
purpose: CP2 is the machine-checkable schema for directional consequence claims. `lab_director` emits forward CP2 at T-n. `oracle_cp2_emitter` emits an hindsight `ideal_cp2` that preserves the same CP2-core fields (`evidence_dictionary`, `epicentre_thesis`, `predictions_t`, `trade_rationale`) and may add an optional `oracle_meta` object. Engine ownership split remains unchanged: the model emits semantic fields including snapshot_price and target_price; the engine injects deterministic temporal fields and audits model-emitted snapshot_price against the subject-run equity baseline. `system.lib.schema_validator.validate_cp2` continues to validate the CP2-core and tolerate additive Oracle-meta fields without weakening grounding or temporal enforcement, while Oracle runtime requires `oracle_meta` plus Oracle-only handle validation for `ideal_cp2` without changing the shared schema.

evidence_dictionary.constraint: Inherited from CP1. Must also satisfy CP2 grounding rules.
evidence_dictionary.semantics: The only admissible grounding set for CP2-core claims. For Oracle, every ledger_id in this array must come from the subject-side T-n admissible set. Truth-side or explanatory refs may not appear here.

epicentre_thesis.constraint: Inherited from CP1 + epicentre_thesis semantic contract.
epicentre_thesis.semantics: Single dominant-force claim. Predictions must be consequences of this thesis. For Oracle, the thesis may be hindsight-informed but it must still be defendable using subject-side grounding only.

predictions_t.constraint: Non-empty. Each entry must be a valid PredictionUnit.
predictions_t.semantics: Directional consequence claims that are explicit consequences of epicentre_thesis, not independent calls. Oracle uses the same field to express the best subject-grounded hindsight reconstruction of what should have been forecast.

trade_rationale.constraint: Word count >= trade_rationale_base_words + trade_rationale_per_prediction_words * len(predictions_t). Must contain only resolved [N] citations.
trade_rationale.semantics: Narrates the force -> transmission -> prediction consequence chain. Not a list of isolated trade ideas. For Oracle, the rationale may reference hindsight only through optional Oracle-meta trace, not by contaminating evidence_dictionary.

oracle_meta.constraint: Optional Oracle-only additive object at the shared-schema level. Present only on hindsight `ideal_cp2`. Never used as admissible grounding and never required for Lab CP2. Oracle runtime requires it when validating `ideal_cp2`.
oracle_meta.semantics: Trace layer recording how realized T evidence informed the hindsight reconstruction without changing the admissibility law for CP2-core fields.

target_id.constraint: Non-empty. For equity predictions: uppercase ticker symbol.
target_id.semantics: The entity being predicted. Must exist in the T-n stock+ETF ticker universe when out-of-universe enforcement is active.

direction.semantics: Expected directional move at horizon T. Must be a consequence of the epicentre thesis transmission path.

snapshot_time.constraint: ISO 8601 datetime string. Engine-owned deterministic field; must equal runtime snapshot anchor.
snapshot_time.semantics: When the T-n snapshot was taken. Enables deterministic evaluation window.

target_time_iso.constraint: ISO 8601 datetime string. Must be after snapshot_time. Engine-owned deterministic field; must equal resolved runtime horizon target.
target_time_iso.semantics: When the prediction resolves (T). Defines the falsification window.

snapshot_price.constraint: Finite numeric baseline price captured at snapshot_time. Model-emitted and engine-audited against T-n stock+ETF feed prices.
snapshot_price.semantics: Baseline for direction evaluation at target_time_iso.

target_price.constraint: Finite numeric projected price level at target_time_iso.
target_price.semantics: The projected price level by target_time_iso. Must be logically consistent with snapshot_price and direction.

invalidation.constraint: Non-empty string. Must state a specific falsifiable condition for this individual prediction target.
invalidation.semantics: Per-prediction invalidation condition. Distinct from the global thesis invalidation in trade_rationale.

confidence.constraint: Optional confidence level for this prediction. When present, must be one of HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW.
confidence.semantics: Strength of evidence supporting this specific prediction target. HIGH = multi-lane convergence with strong thesis link. MEDIUM = directional agreement with some uncertainty. LOW = single-lane or weak thesis link. Evolve uses this for learning: which confidence levels track actual hit rates.

transmission_path.constraint: Optional. When present, must name the specific causal mechanism from epicentre_thesis to this target.
transmission_path.semantics: Explicit link between the dominant force and this prediction. Improves comparability between Lab and Oracle CP2 and enables Evolve to track which transmission paths succeed or fail.

truth_influence_refs.constraint: Optional list of truth-side or explanatory refs that influenced the hindsight reconstruction. When present in Oracle runtime, each entry must match `<artifact>:<identifier>` with exactly one colon.
truth_influence_refs.semantics: These refs may explain why Oracle changed the thesis or target mix, but they are not admissible grounding.

realized_diff_refs.constraint: Optional refs into deterministic Oracle diff artifacts such as prediction_reconciliation. When present in Oracle runtime, each entry must match `<artifact>:<identifier>` with exactly one colon.
realized_diff_refs.semantics: Preserves which realized comparisons materially shaped the hindsight reconstruction.

explicit_exclusions.constraint: Optional non-empty objects when a realized driver influenced reasoning but had to be excluded from grounding.
explicit_exclusions.semantics: Makes the grounding firewall inspectable. Each exclusion says what was seen, why it was relevant, and why it was not admissible.

oracle_hindsight_notes.constraint: Optional concise notes. No new grounding claims, ledger ids, or targets may be introduced here.
oracle_hindsight_notes.semantics: Freeform hindsight trace for auditability and critique authoring.

kind.constraint: Short classifier such as TRUTH_ONLY_SIGNAL, WEAK_SUBJECT_SUPPORT, or NON_COMPARABLE_MACRO.
kind.semantics: Why the item was excluded from CP2-core grounding.

ref.constraint: Non-empty Oracle handle matching `<artifact>:<identifier>` with exactly one colon.
ref.semantics: The excluded truth-side or contextual reference. Oracle runtime validates format only and does not perform existence lookup.

reason.semantics: Narrates why the excluded item informed hindsight but could not ground the final CP2-core.

thesis.question: Was the dominant-force claim itself correct, incomplete, or overfit?

prediction.question: Were the chosen targets, directions, and projected outcomes the right consequences of the thesis?

prediction_justification.question: Did the rationale correctly connect evidence -> force -> transmission -> target?

citations_grounding.question: Did the grounding set contain the right subject-side support, and only admissible support?

oracle_meta.question: Which realized-truth inputs influenced hindsight reconstruction without becoming grounding?

oracle_contract_model.description: Oracle does not get a second CP2 schema. It reuses the same CP2-core and may append optional hindsight trace.
oracle_contract_model.validator_tolerance: validate_cp2 enforces CP2-core and ignores additive Oracle-meta keys; Oracle runtime layers a stricter validator that requires oracle_meta on ideal_cp2 and validates Oracle handle syntax without changing the shared schema.
grounding_law: Only subject-side T-n ledger ids may appear in evidence_dictionary.
Truth-side refs may orient reconstruction but must live outside evidence_dictionary.
Oracle-meta is explanatory trace, not admissible support.
Golden-ID enforcement still applies to the CP2-core evidence_dictionary.
Oracle handle fields use format only `<artifact>:<identifier>` with exactly one colon and no existence lookup.

consequence_coherence.description: CP2 predictions are not independent calls. They must be explicit consequences of epicentre_thesis.
rules: epicentre_thesis declares one dominant force at T-n.
Each predictions_t[i] is explainable as a transmission consequence of that force.
trade_rationale must narrate force -> transmission -> prediction consequence chain.
If a prediction depends on an alternative driver, that alternative must be subordinated to (or shown as derivative of) the epicentre force — not introduced as a second co-equal thesis.

grounding_rule.description: Every prediction must be grounded in explicitly sourced equity lane data.
grounding_rule.requirement: For every PredictionUnit in predictions_t, there must exist at least one evidence_dictionary entry where: subject == target_id (case-normalized .upper().strip()) AND ledger_id starts with 'S_' or 'E_'.
grounding_rule.rationale: A prediction that references only MACRO or NEWS evidence without a stock/ETF grounding entry is a grounding failure. Oracle-meta never satisfies this rule.

out_of_universe.description: When subject_run_dir is provided, every target_id must exist in the T-n stock+ETF ticker universe.
out_of_universe.fallback: If ticker universe cannot be loaded, MISSING_CONTEXT is emitted and OUT_OF_UNIVERSE check is skipped (degraded mode).

oracle_golden_id_guard.description: Oracle runs add an additional invariant over the CP2-core grounding set.
oracle_golden_id_guard.requirement: Every ledger_id in evidence_dictionary must exist in the T-n snapshot ledger set from the corresponding Lab run.
oracle_golden_id_guard.enforcement: validate_golden_ids(evidence_dictionary, subject_run_dir) — called from engine Oracle path, not from validate_cp2 itself.

citation_rules.description: Same rules as CP1 epicentre_thesis, applied to both epicentre_thesis and trade_rationale.
citation_rules.valid_format: [N] where N is a positive integer
citation_rules.resolution_rule: Every [N] must resolve to an evidence_dictionary entry with matching ref_id.

validation_sequence: 1. validate_cp1(payload) — structural + citation + word-count for CP1 fields

2. Validate CP2-specific fields — predictions_t presence, direction enum, snapshot_price + target_price numeric validity, direction/target coherence, temporal fields, trade_rationale, and engine price-audit flags
3. Grounding checks — per-prediction evidence with S_ or E_ ledger
5. [Oracle only] Golden ID guard — Phase 10, engine-level
6. [Oracle ideal_cp2 only] Require oracle_meta and validate Oracle handle syntax for truth_influence_refs, realized_diff_refs, and explicit_exclusions[].ref
7. Ignore additive Oracle-meta keys outside Oracle-specific validation

error_codes._inherited: All CP1 error codes apply (MISSING_FIELD, INVALID_REF_ID, DUPLICATE_REF_ID, DUPLICATE_LEDGER_ID, INVALID_LEDGER_ID, MALFORMED_CITATION, CITATION_UNRESOLVED, BELOW_MIN_WORD_COUNT).
error_codes.INVALID_FIELD: A field value is present but invalid (e.g. direction not in {UP, DOWN}).
error_codes.MISSING_SNAPSHOT_PRICE: snapshot_price is missing for a prediction target.
error_codes.MISSING_TARGET_PRICE: target_price is missing for a prediction target.
error_codes.INVALID_TARGET_DIRECTION: direction and target_price are not logically consistent with snapshot_price.
error_codes.SNAPSHOT_PRICE_MISMATCH: Model-emitted snapshot_price diverges from feed baseline beyond allowed tolerance.
error_codes.TEMPORAL_ORDER_INVALID: target_time_iso is not strictly after snapshot_time.
error_codes.TEMPORAL_MISMATCH: prediction temporal fields diverge from canonical runtime values.
error_codes.GROUNDING_FAILED_WRONG_LANE: A target_id has no evidence entry with subject == target_id AND ledger_id starting with S_ or E_.
error_codes.UNGROUNDED_TARGET: A target_id is not present in the cross_corr_v2 TARGET_SWARM or SOLO_TARGET sets. Prediction was not structurally grounded by the cross-correlator.
error_codes.MISSING_CONTEXT: Ticker universe could not be loaded from subject_run_dir.
error_codes.OUT_OF_UNIVERSE: A target_id is not present in the T-n ticker set.

lab_director.role: Extend locked CP1 thesis with forward predictions and trade rationale.
lab_director.boundary: Must ensure predictions_t are direct consequences of epicentre_thesis, emit snapshot_price + target_price per prediction, ground targets with subject-matching stock or ETF evidence, and must not introduce a second co-equal thesis.

oracle_cp2_emitter.role: Emit hindsight `ideal_cp2` using the same CP2-core plus Oracle-meta trace that is required by Oracle runtime.
oracle_cp2_emitter.boundary: Must preserve the full CP2-core contract, obey golden ID enforcement, keep grounding strictly subject-side, place all truth-side influence outside evidence_dictionary, and include oracle_meta for Oracle runtime using format-only `<artifact>:<identifier>` handles.

deferred_contracts.scorecard.md: LAYERED/DEFERRED: depends on confidence/magnitude semantics removed from v0.
deferred_contracts.counter_vector.md: Non-normative prose guide only; not validated by engine.
deferred_contracts.hypothesis_slug.md: Non-normative prose guide only; not validated by engine.
